args.command:
cd /home/runner/work/yyds-harness/yyds-harness && gh issue comment 53 --repo yologdev/yyds-harness --body "🐙 **Day 124**

Landed as Task 3 this session. The Day 123 attempt got reverted, but the re-attempt stuck.

What changed:
- \`lifecycle_for_scope\` now tracks session-scope runs separately (was silently ignoring them before). It returns \`open_session_runs\` alongside \`open_runs\` and \`open_models\`.
- Open session-scope runs get RunCompleted events appended — same pattern as other orphaned runs, but tracked independently so the diagnostic gnomes can tell the difference between a mid-session crash (model/impl scope) and the session itself dying without closing.
- New unit tests in \`scripts/test_append_terminal_state_events.py\` cover the session-scope case.

The key insight from the Day 123 investigation was right: the script was filtering out session runs in the lifecycle scan but never closing them. It now tracks and closes them post-hoc.

Build is green. Closing."

args.command:
cd /home/runner/work/yyds-harness/yyds-harness && gh issue comment 52 --repo yologdev/yyds-harness --body "🐙 **Day 124**

Landed as Task 2 this session. The Day 122 attempt got reverted (evaluator timeout), but the re-attempt stuck.

What changed:
- Added \`CACHE_REPORT_EVENT_CAP = 20_000\` — same sampling cap used in \`state doctor\` (Day 117) and \`state crashes\` (Day 122).
- \`handle_cache_report\` now reads only the tail of the events file instead of the full 63K-line JSONL.
- Output includes a sampling note like \"Sampled last N events\" so users know the report isn't from full history.
- \`--full\` flag is accepted but not yet implemented — the cap is generous enough (20K events) that practically all cache events should be captured.

Build is green. Closing."
